result.summary: GitHub exposes a REST API at `https://api.github.com`, a GraphQL API at `https://api.github.com/graphql`, and the `gh` CLI; authentication is via personal access tokens, GitHub Apps, or OAuth apps depending on surface.
result.description: GitHub is a source code hosting and collaboration platform for repositories, issues, pull requests, automation, and related developer workflows. It also provides enterprise and self-hosted variants of its platform.

github_pat.label: GitHub personal access token
github_pat.setup: Create a token in [Personal access tokens settings](https://github.com/settings/tokens). GitHub supports fine-grained and classic PATs; for API use, choose the scopes or repository permissions your integration needs as described in [Managing your personal access tokens](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens).

github_app.label: GitHub App credentials
github_app.setup: Register an app in [GitHub App settings](https://github.com/settings/apps) using [Registering a GitHub App](https://docs.github.com/en/apps/creating-github-apps/registering-a-github-app/registering-a-github-app). After registration, note the app ID and generate a private key; use those to create a JWT, then exchange it for an installation access token or user access token as needed per [GitHub App authentication docs](https://docs.github.com/en/apps/creating-github-apps/authenticating-with-a-github-app/about-authentication-with-a-github-app).

github_oauth_app.label: GitHub OAuth app credentials
github_oauth_app.setup: Create an OAuth app in [Developer settings](https://github.com/settings/developers) following [Creating an OAuth app](https://docs.github.com/en/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/creating-an-oauth-app). Your app gets a client ID and client secret; send users through the GitHub OAuth flow to obtain user access tokens for API calls.
